The Replit Prompt (Copy & Paste)

"I need to build a high-conversion, minimalist 'Pitch' page (pitch.html) that functions as a filter for my 'Contrarian Builder' thesis.

1. Page Architecture:

Create a new file pitch.html using the page layout.
The header should be a single, brutalist line: 'DO NOT PITCH UNLESS YOU ARE RIGHT—BUT CURRENTLY ALONE.'
2. The Form UI:

Style the form with a 'Terminal' or 'Code Editor' aesthetic: Dark background (#050505), subtle monospaced fonts (like JetBrains Mono or Geist Mono), and input fields that are just bottom-borders (1px solid #333).
Focus state: When a user clicks an input, the border should glow with my accent color.
3. The 'Filter' Questions (Fields):

Who: Name & Email.
The Proof: 'GitHub / Project URL' (Mark as required).
The Non-Consensus: 'What do you believe about your sector that everyone else thinks is crazy?' (Large textarea).
The Primitive: A dropdown/select: 'Are you building a Feature, a Product, or a Protocol?'
The Inevitability: 'Why is this technically inevitable?' (Large textarea).
4. Technical Integration:

Set up the form action to point to a placeholder (or Formspree/Tally if I provide the ID later).
Add a 'Submit' button that is a simple, high-contrast white block with black text. On hover, it should invert or have a slight 'glitch' effect.
5. Success State:

Once submitted, hide the form and display a single line of text: 'Acknowledged. If there is resonance, you will hear from me.'
Please generate the HTML and the specific SCSS for this page now."

Why this works for your sectors:

The GitHub Requirement: In Cybersecurity, Infra, and Blockchain, code is the only truth. By making the URL mandatory, you immediately filter out "idea guys" without a technical foundation.
The "Crazy" Question: This directly tests your thesis. If their answer is "I think AI is going to be big," they aren't a contrarian. If their answer is "I think encryption as we know it will be obsolete by 2027 because of X," they are a fit.
The "Protocol" Dropdown: This forces the founder to categorize their ambition. You mentioned focusing on Digital Infrastructure and Blockchain; you are looking for people building systems, not just apps.
Minimalist Friction: The brutalist "Do not pitch unless..." header acts as a psychological gatekeeper. It signals that your time is valuable and your conviction is high.
Final Polish for your Jekyll Repo

Once Replit generates the code:

The _data/navigation.yml: Make sure the "Pitch" link is the only one highlighted (perhaps with a border or a different color) in your nav bar.
The "Digital Art" Angle: Since you also cover Digital Art and Entertainment, I suggest adding a small "File Upload" field for a "Vision Deck" or "Technical Spec," but keep it optional. The code is more important.
